checks.summary: The diff satisfies the spec by avoiding invocation of an empty KillTimer callback while preserving callback invocation for non-empty callbacks and preserving the abort path.

evidence.label: Empty callback guarded
evidence.detail: In tests/st/kill_timer.h, the previous unconditional func() call on timeout is replaced with if (func) { func(); }, so a default empty std::function is not invoked before abort().

evidence.label: Timeout behavior preserved
evidence.detail: The existing LOG(INFO) diagnostic remains before the guarded callback and abort() remains immediately after it, so non-empty callbacks are still invoked once before abort() and empty callbacks fall through to abort().

evidence.label: Normal destruction unchanged
evidence.detail: The diff only changes the !finished timeout branch; no code in the normal finished/destruction path is modified.

checks.summary: The diff follows the plan exactly: it wraps func() in an if (func) guard and leaves the timeout log plus abort unchanged.

evidence.label: Guard added in watchdog lambda
evidence.detail: In tests/st/kill_timer.h, func() is now enclosed by if (func) { ... } inside the timeout branch.

evidence.label: Diagnostic and abort unchanged
evidence.detail: LOG(INFO) << "Time Out, Need to Kill" remains before the callback site, and abort(); remains after the guarded callback.
